name: Boolawnee ( Fried Leek Pastries )
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour and salt into a bowl. Make a well in the center and add cold water. Mix to form a firm dough, kneading for about 5 minutes until elastic. Dust with additional flour if needed. Wrap in plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.
2. Cut most of the green tops from the leeks. Halve them lengthwise, rinse well to remove soil between leaves, remove roots, and dry on paper towels. Dice into 1/4 inch (5 mm) intervals, then across to measure out 2 cups. Add salt and chili pepper, kneading with hands to soften.
3. Stir in oil. Roll pieces of dough into balls the size of a large hazelnut, then roll thinly into circles about 4 inches (10 cm) in diameter. Alternatively, roll out the dough and cut into rounds.
4. Place about 2 teaspoons of leek filling in the center of each circle. Moisten half of the pastry edge around the circle's edge, fold over the filling, and press the edge to seal well.
5. Make small crescent-shaped marks or press with a fork around the edge. Fry 3-4 at a time in hot oil until golden brown, turning to ensure even browning.
6. Drain on paper towels and serve hot or warm.
